title: AI Identity Fraud: Detect and Defend

modules.title: Module 1: The Identity Fraud Explosion — What AI Changed and Why It Matters Now
modules.sub: Understanding how AI reshaped the identity fraud threat landscape
modules.description: Learners examine the scale and velocity of modern AI-enabled identity fraud compared to pre-AI methods, tracing how generative tools lowered the barrier for attackers. They assess real-world case studies to recognize why traditional defenses are no longer sufficient and why urgency around this threat is justified.

modules.title: Module 2: How Synthetic Identities Are Built Using AI-Generated Documents and Data
modules.sub: Tracing the construction of convincing fake identities from scratch
modules.description: Learners walk through the attacker's workflow for assembling synthetic identities, from AI-generated faces and fabricated documents to stitched personal data profiles. By understanding each construction step, learners can identify the artifacts and inconsistencies these methods leave behind that verification systems must catch.

modules.title: Module 3: Account Takeover — AI-Assisted Credential Stuffing and Social Engineering
modules.sub: Recognizing AI-powered tactics that hijack legitimate accounts
modules.description: Learners analyze how attackers combine credential stuffing automation with AI-generated phishing, voice cloning, and targeted social engineering to seize existing accounts. They evaluate scenarios to distinguish normal account activity from takeover indicators and identify intervention points for both users and security teams.

modules.title: Module 4: Identity Verification Technologies and Their Current Limitations
modules.sub: Evaluating what verification tools can and cannot reliably catch
modules.description: Learners survey the major categories of identity verification technology — document checks, biometric matching, liveness detection, and behavioral signals — and stress-test each against known AI attack vectors. They leave with a clear map of where current tools hold, where they fail, and what compensating controls can fill the gaps.

modules.title: Module 5: Protecting Your Own Digital Identity — Practical Steps for Every User
modules.sub: Taking personal action to reduce individual identity fraud exposure
modules.description: Learners build a personal identity protection checklist covering credential hygiene, multi-factor authentication choices, data minimization, and monitoring services appropriate for non-technical users. They prioritize actions by risk impact so they finish the module with a concrete, immediately actionable hardening plan.

modules.title: Module 6: Organizational Identity Proofing — KYC, Liveness Detection, and Risk Scoring
modules.sub: Designing layered identity controls that withstand AI-driven attacks
modules.description: Learners examine how organizations implement Know Your Customer workflows, liveness detection pipelines, and dynamic risk scoring to detect synthetic and stolen identities at onboarding and beyond. They evaluate control configurations against realistic attack scenarios and recommend enhancements suited to different organizational risk profiles and technical capabilities.

modules.title: Module 7: Policy, Regulation, and the Future of AI-Resistant Identity Systems
modules.sub: Navigating emerging rules and next-generation identity assurance frameworks
modules.description: Learners review the current regulatory landscape — including digital identity frameworks, AML obligations, and emerging AI governance requirements — and assess how they shape organizational identity proofing duties. They then explore emerging standards such as verifiable credentials and decentralized identity to evaluate which approaches show the most promise for staying ahead of AI-enabled fraud.

synopsis: AI now enables criminals to generate convincing synthetic identities, forge documents, bypass liveness checks, and impersonate real people at industrial scale — exposing individuals, financial institutions, and enterprises to fraud losses that are growing year over year. This course explores how AI-enabled identity fraud works from both the attacker's and defender's perspective, giving users and professionals the knowledge to harden verification practices before an identity is stolen or exploited.
rationale: Identity verification systems deployed before 2023 were not designed to contend with AI-generated synthetic media and document forgery, leaving a dangerous gap between current fraud capabilities and organizational defenses.
learning_outcome: Learners can recognize the signals of AI-assisted identity fraud targeting themselves or their organization and evaluate or implement identity-proofing controls appropriate to their level of technical access and risk exposure.
